name: AI Assistants
description: Educators are reimagining what a teaching assistant can be. These AI-powered tools handle everything from lesson scaffolding to real-time student support — each one reflecting a different vision of how intelligence can serve the classroom.

apps.name: Intercultural Chapter Assistant
apps.description: Students and educators explore intercultural concepts and navigate cross-cultural communication with AI guidance. The tool helps bridge cultural understanding gaps that textbooks often oversimplify.
apps.creator: Kali Moore
apps.usage: Students and educators explore intercultural concepts and navigate cross-cultural communication with AI guidance. The tool helps bridge cultural understanding gaps that textbooks often oversimplify.
apps.impact: Helped people navigate the nuances of intercultural communication that no textbook can fully capture — the real, messy, beautiful complexity of working across cultures.

apps.name: Discover TFA Regions Chatbot
apps.description: Prospective TFA corps members explore different placement regions, learning about the communities, schools, and challenges they'd encounter in each location.
apps.usage: Prospective TFA corps members explore different placement regions, learning about the communities, schools, and challenges they'd encounter in each location. The tool makes the overwhelming placement decision feel more informed and personal.
apps.impact: Helped prospective corps members make one of the biggest decisions of their early career — choosing where to teach — with confidence rather than guesswork, by giving them a real sense of each community.

apps.name: TFA Ignite Assistant
apps.description: TFA Ignite participants get program support and resource navigation during their intensive preparation period. The tool supplements human mentorship with always-available AI guidance.
apps.usage: TFA Ignite participants get program support and resource navigation during their intensive preparation period. The tool supplements human mentorship with always-available AI guidance.
apps.impact: Supplemented the intensive human mentorship of TFA Ignite with AI support that's available at 2 AM when lesson plans are due and anxiety is high — the moments when new teachers need support most.

apps.name: Knowledge Management Helper
apps.description: Organizations use this to organize and surface institutional knowledge that would otherwise live in one person's head or get lost in email threads. The tool ensures critical information is always accessible.
apps.usage: Organizations use this to organize and surface institutional knowledge that would otherwise live in one person's head or get lost in email threads. The tool ensures critical information is always accessible.
apps.impact: Captured the institutional knowledge that walks out the door every time an employee leaves — organizations stopped losing hard-won wisdom and started building on it.

name: Amplify
description: Amplify educators have taken their deep curriculum knowledge and built something new with it. These apps extend and personalize the Amplify experience in ways only the people closest to the work could imagine.

apps.name: Amplify Grade 7 Unit 4 Review
apps.description: Amplify Grade 7 science review
apps.usage: Seventh-grade science students use this app to review key concepts from Amplify Unit 4 before assessments. Teachers assign it as a self-paced study tool that walks students through vocabulary, core ideas, and practice questions aligned to the Amplify curriculum, helping them identify gaps before test day.
apps.impact: Transformed test prep into structured, confident review

apps.name: Amplify Grade 7 Tutor
apps.description: AI tutor for Amplify Grade 7
apps.usage: Seventh graders working through the Amplify science curriculum use this tutor for extra support on challenging concepts. Students ask questions about specific lessons, get step-by-step explanations of phenomena, and work through practice problems with guided feedback tailored to Amplify's investigation-based approach.
apps.impact: Students who previously struggled with Amplify's inquiry-based format gained confidence by getting patient, on-demand explanations. Teachers reported fewer repeated questions during class, freeing them to focus on deeper discussions.

apps.name: Amplify Grade 8 Unit 3 Review
apps.description: 8th graders prepare for Amplify science unit assessments with structured review that builds genuine understanding rather than encouraging last-minute memorization.
apps.usage: 8th graders prepare for Amplify science unit assessments with structured review that builds genuine understanding rather than encouraging last-minute memorization. The tool guides students through the key concepts and skills each unit demands.
apps.impact: Gave 8th graders structured review that builds real understanding — students who used to cram the night before started reviewing with purpose because the tool showed them exactly what they needed to know and why.

apps.name: Amplify Math Study Buddy
apps.description: Students study Amplify Math concepts with a dedicated AI buddy that provides extra practice beyond what the curriculum's built-in activities offer.
apps.usage: Students study Amplify Math concepts with a dedicated AI buddy that provides extra practice beyond what the curriculum's built-in activities offer. The tool fills the gap for students who need more repetitions to achieve mastery.
apps.impact: Created a reliable study partner for students who need extra practice — the kind of patient, always-available support that makes the difference between almost understanding and truly mastering a concept.

apps.name: Compass Rose Ingenuity 5th grade Amplify ELA
apps.description: Compass Rose Ingenuity 5th graders get Amplify ELA support customized for their specific school context and student population. The tool bridges the gap between a national curriculum and local classroom needs.
apps.usage: Compass Rose Ingenuity 5th graders get Amplify ELA support customized for their specific school context and student population. The tool bridges the gap between a national curriculum and local classroom needs.
apps.impact: Showed that national curriculum materials work best when they're locally adapted — Compass Rose students got Amplify's rigor with their school's personality baked in.

apps.name: Amplify ExitTicket Quiz CREATOR
apps.description: Teachers create quick exit tickets and quizzes for Amplify lessons in minutes, checking for understanding at the end of every class.
apps.usage: Teachers create quick exit tickets and quizzes for Amplify lessons in minutes, checking for understanding at the end of every class. The tool generates questions that align to the specific learning goals of each Amplify lesson.
apps.impact: Gave teachers a quick way to check understanding at the end of every Amplify lesson — the daily pulse check that turns teaching from "I think they got it" into "I know exactly who needs what tomorrow."

name: Arts & Design
description: Where creativity meets craft. Educators and students have built tools that open up visual arts, design thinking, and creative expression — proving that art education thrives when learners can shape their own tools.

apps.name: Year 1 - Art and Design Foundation
apps.description: Year 1 students learn foundational art concepts
apps.creator: T-TEL
apps.usage: Year 1 students learn foundational art concepts
apps.impact: Brought structured art education to classrooms where it was previously absent

apps.name: Year 1 - Art and Design Studio
apps.description: Year 1 students engage in creative studio projects with curriculum support that balances artistic freedom with intentional skill development.
apps.creator: T-TEL
apps.usage: Year 1 students engage in creative studio projects with curriculum support that balances artistic freedom with intentional skill development. Teachers use it to structure studio time that builds technique alongside creativity.
apps.impact: Connected studio practice to intentional skill development — students approach art-making with both creativity and technique, producing work they're increasingly proud of as their skills grow.

apps.name: Music Lyric Generator
apps.description: Students compose and experiment with lyrics
apps.creator: Ron Mateo
apps.usage: Students in music and creative writing classes use this app to generate original song lyrics based on themes, moods, and genres they choose. Teachers use it as a starting point for songwriting workshops, helping students understand lyrical structure, rhyme schemes, and emotional expression through music.
apps.impact: Opened songwriting to students who thought they were not creative enough, producing original work that students performed at school events and talent shows.

apps.name: CREATE - Arts Integration
apps.description: Teachers connect visual arts with other subject areas, designing lessons where art isn't an add-on but a vehicle for deeper understanding of academic content.
apps.creator: Regina Smithers
apps.usage: Teachers connect visual arts with other subject areas, designing lessons where art isn't an add-on but a vehicle for deeper understanding of academic content. The tool shows how arts integration strengthens both the art and the content.
apps.impact: Helped teachers see arts integration as a teaching strategy rather than a fun Friday activity — students learned science through sculpture and history through visual storytelling, with both the art and academics stronger for it.

apps.name: Ludwig Van Beethoven's life and music
apps.description: Students explore Beethoven's life and works
apps.creator: Alaina Turner
apps.usage: Music appreciation students explore Beethoven's biography, musical periods, and major compositions through an interactive conversation. The app covers his early training in Bonn, his Vienna years, his struggle with hearing loss, and how each phase shaped his compositions.
apps.impact: Made classical music history feel personal and accessible. Students who initially saw Beethoven as distant and irrelevant found themselves genuinely curious about his life and motivated to listen more deeply.

apps.name: Start My Art
apps.description: Students who feel intimidated by creating art get confidence-building guidance to start their first projects.
apps.usage: Students who feel intimidated by creating art get confidence-building guidance to start their first projects. The tool removes the paralysis of the blank canvas by providing starting points that spark rather than constrain creativity.
apps.impact: Removed the paralyzing "I can't draw" mindset — students who had never considered themselves artists started creating with confidence because the tool gave them a way in.

apps.name: Theatre Arts Elective Project Development
apps.description: Drama and theatre students develop original performance projects from concept through production, getting AI support for scriptwriting, staging, and creative direction.
apps.creator: Graham Decarte
apps.usage: Drama and theatre students develop original performance projects from concept through production, getting AI support for scriptwriting, staging, and creative direction. The tool helps translate creative vision into producible performances.
apps.impact: Empowered drama students to become playwrights and directors, not just performers — they went from interpreting others' scripts to creating their own original works.

apps.name: 3D Design Helper
apps.description: Students explore 3D modeling concepts and techniques with an AI guide that breaks down the technical barriers to digital design. The tool focuses on creative problem-solving rather than software mechanics.
apps.creator: Nathan Weber
apps.usage: Students explore 3D modeling concepts and techniques with an AI guide that breaks down the technical barriers to digital design. The tool focuses on creative problem-solving rather than software mechanics.
apps.impact: Lowered the technical barrier to 3D design so students could focus on what they actually want to create — their ideas stopped being limited by their software skills.

name: Assessment & Feedback
description: The community has built dozens of different approaches to understanding what students know. From quick formative checks to deep rubric-based feedback, each tool reflects a different classroom reality and a different philosophy of assessment.

apps.name: SAQ Tutor
apps.description: Students practice structured SAQ responses
apps.usage: Students preparing for AP and standardized exams practice short answer questions with targeted feedback on their responses. The tutor evaluates answers for completeness, accuracy, and use of evidence, helping students learn what graders look for.
apps.impact: Closed the gap between students who could discuss ideas verbally and those who could put them on paper under timed conditions. Practice scores improved as students internalized the structure of strong short answers.

apps.name: Critical Minds
apps.description: Critical thinking tool
apps.usage: Fairfax County students use Critical Minds to practice evaluating arguments, identifying logical fallacies, and constructing evidence-based counterpoints. The app presents claims on current issues and asks students to analyze the reasoning, evidence quality, and potential biases.
apps.impact: Built a generation of more skeptical, thoughtful readers in Fairfax schools. Students began applying critical analysis skills spontaneously to news articles and social media claims.

apps.name: Know-Show Chart Generator SY2526
apps.description: Standards-based assessment planning
apps.usage: Teachers paste a standard or learning objective and the app generates a Know-Show chart breaking down what students need to know and how they can show their understanding. Saves hours of backwards planning by automating the initial analysis of standards into observable outcomes.
apps.impact: Standardized how teachers across grade levels decompose standards into assessable components. New teachers especially benefited from seeing clear examples of what mastery looks like.

apps.name: Algebra I Midterm Review
apps.description: Students review key concepts and practice for midterms
apps.usage: Algebra I students work through a comprehensive midterm review covering equations, inequalities, functions, and graphing. The app generates practice problems at varying difficulty levels and provides worked solutions that show each step, helping students identify exactly where their understanding breaks down.
apps.impact: Transformed midterm prep from passive textbook review into active, guided practice

apps.name: Corbin, Your Feedback Sidekick
apps.description: Teachers and students use Corbin to get competency-aligned feedback on student work evidence.
apps.creator: Thomas Gaffey
apps.usage: Teachers and students use Corbin to get competency-aligned feedback on student work evidence. The tool evaluates student submissions against specific competency indicators and provides targeted, actionable feedback that helps students understand exactly where they stand and what to work on next.
apps.impact: Made competency-based feedback feel personal and immediate rather than abstract and delayed — students who used to see rubrics as mysterious scoring tools started seeing them as roadmaps for growth.

apps.name: Nashville Classical Testing - Intellectual Prep
apps.description: Teachers build intellectually rigorous test materials
apps.usage: Teachers at Nashville Classical prepare intellectually rigorous assessments by working through the content themselves with AI support. The app helps anticipate student misconceptions, identify the key understandings each question targets, and ensure assessments measure deep comprehension rather than surface recall.
apps.impact: Raised the bar on assessment quality across the school. Teachers reported that the intellectual prep process itself deepened their own content knowledge and improved their classroom instruction.

apps.name: Assessment Text Converter Student Version v2
apps.description: Converts assessments to accessible formats
apps.usage: Students with reading difficulties or English language learners use this tool to access assessment texts at their comprehension level without losing the rigor of the original content. The app adjusts vocabulary complexity and sentence structure while preserving the core concepts being assessed.
apps.impact: Leveled the playing field for diverse learners by ensuring assessments measured content knowledge rather than reading ability. Teachers saw more accurate data about what students actually understood.

apps.name: The MBU Rubric Generator
apps.description: Rubric generator
apps.usage: Teachers generate rubrics in minutes
apps.impact: Produced rubrics in minutes that previously took hours

apps.name: Elevating Your Speaking
apps.description: A speaking practice bot that coaches multilingual learners on English proficiency exam skills — pronunciation, pacing, and fluency patterns that automated scoring systems recognize.
apps.creator: Dr. Elvira Salazar
apps.role: National Director of Online Learning and Technology
apps.usage: Multilingual students practice speaking skills for proficiency exams with AI coaching on pause, enunciation, and cadence. The bot provides frequent, low-stakes practice that one teacher managing 25 diverse students can't offer consistently. One educator has already shared it with her school district for replication.
apps.impact: Gives multilingual learners a fighting chance against automated scoring systems trained primarily on native English speakers. Instead of waiting for policy changes, educators are using AI to help students navigate a system not designed for them — while advocating for better technology that reflects their communities.

apps.name: Unit 1 Literary Analysis Essay Feedback Partner
apps.description: Rubric-aligned literary analysis feedback
apps.usage: ELL students at International HS at Union Square submit literary analysis drafts and receive detailed feedback on thesis development, textual evidence integration, analysis depth, and academic language. The app understands common patterns in multilingual student writing and provides culturally responsive guidance.
apps.impact: Gave multilingual learners the specific, patient feedback they need to develop academic writing skills in English, with measurable improvement in essay scores across the unit.

apps.impact: Provided always-available tutoring support for a course where students often struggle with the cumulative nature of the material and fall behind without timely help.

name: CA Community Colleges
description: California's community college educators are building for a student population unlike any other — working adults, first-generation learners, career changers. These apps reflect that diversity and the creativity it demands.

apps.name: PIQs For Transfer Students
apps.description: UC transfer PIQ support
apps.usage: Community college students preparing to transfer to four-year universities use this app to draft and refine their Personal Insight Questions. The app guides students through brainstorming, structuring their responses, and revising for clarity and authenticity.
apps.impact: Democratized access to college application coaching for community college students who often lack the counseling support available at four-year feeder high schools.

apps.name: FAFSA Assistant
apps.description: FAFSA application guide
apps.usage: Students and families navigating the FAFSA process use this assistant to understand each question, gather the right documents, and avoid common mistakes that delay financial aid. The app walks users through the form step by step in plain language.
apps.impact: Reduced FAFSA completion barriers for first-generation college students and families who found the form intimidating, increasing the number of students who successfully secured financial aid.

apps.name: Biology 122: Glendale Community College

apps.usage: Students in Biology 122 at Glendale Community College use this app to review lecture concepts, prepare for lab practicals, and work through practice questions aligned to their specific course content and textbook.

apps.impact: Extended the reach of overburdened EOPS counselors, ensuring that more students received timely, informed guidance about their academic and financial aid options.
